jokes.text: is he making this up as he goes along? He's telling an American story. I thought he was gonna do his hit. I think he's doing his bong hit.
jokes.setup: Beck is performing an odd, rambling song and someone asks if he's improvising
jokes.punchline: The mishearing of 'hit song' as 'bong hit' plays on Beck's countercultural image
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that fits the show's observational style about alternative music culture

dialogue.line: is he making this up as he goes along?
He's telling an American story.
I thought he was gonna do his hit.
I think he's doing his bong hit.

jokes.text: So my agent's fucking my booker? Yes. Oh, good to know.
jokes.setup: Larry discovers his agent Stevie is dating his booker Paula, creating a conflict of interest
jokes.punchline: Larry's deadpan 'good to know' reaction to learning about this problematic situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Larry's understated reaction to a genuinely problematic situation is both funny and true to character

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: So my agent's fucking my booker?

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Oh, good to know.

jokes.text: but not a hillbilly from outer space
jokes.setup: Paula defends booking Beck as fresh talent
jokes.punchline: Larry's dismissive characterization of Beck as a 'hillbilly from outer space'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant, quotable description that captures both Larry's snobbishness and Beck's aesthetic perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: but not a hillbilly from outer space

jokes.text: Granted, he's no John tesh
jokes.setup: Paula is defending Beck's artistic merit
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically comparing Beck to John Tesh, the antithesis of cool
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference joke that plays on the contrast between alternative and mainstream

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Granted, he's no John tesh

jokes.text: I love Sally, but I've seen miss struthers up here one more time this month.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about Paula booking Stevie's clients
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific complaint about Sally Struthers appearing too frequently
jokes.explanation: Specific reference works as industry inside joke about booking less glamorous celebrities

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I love Sally, but I've seen miss struthers up here one more time this month.

jokes.text: Well, you know, my hooker days are over.
jokes.setup: Stevie offers to get Paula 'Hole' (the band)
jokes.punchline: Paula mishears and responds as if he offered her prostitutes
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke that works on multiple levels - the mishearing creates a perfect setup/punchline structure

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Well, you know, my hooker days are over.

jokes.text: Ha ha! You are the king! King Kong! Ha ha ha ha!
jokes.setup: Paula makes the hooker/Hole joke
jokes.punchline: Stevie's over-the-top, ass-kissing reaction with the King Kong reference
jokes.explanation: Classic sycophantic agent behavior that establishes Stevie's character - cringey but authentic

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Ha ha! You are the king! King Kong! Ha ha ha ha!

jokes.text: Which ones? The ones who can act.
jokes.setup: Paula asks which Friends cast members Stevie represents
jokes.punchline: His dismissive response implying half the cast can't act
jokes.explanation: Sharp industry insider joke with perfect economy - brutal but funny assessment of the Friends cast

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Which ones? The ones who can act.

jokes.text: The little guy, the hyper guy? Mm-hmm. I don't think so. He's more of a conan o'brien act. Conan won't put him on.
jokes.setup: Stevie pitches comedian Andy Kindler
jokes.punchline: The circular logic that he's perfect for Conan but Conan won't book him
jokes.explanation: Perfect showbiz logic - captures the catch-22 of comedy booking with sharp irony

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: The little guy, the hyper guy? Mm-hmm. I don't think so. He's more of a conan o'brien act. Conan won't put him on.

jokes.text: About American soldier push big elephant out of plane. Very funny.
jokes.setup: Peng Lee is reviewing Operation Dumbo Drop
jokes.punchline: His broken English description of the plot makes it sound absurd
jokes.explanation: Problematic humor that relies on accent/language barrier for laughs - dated and borderline offensive

dialogue.speaker: Peng Lee
dialogue.line: About American soldier push big elephant out of plane. Very funny.

jokes.text: I give it 3 fortune... 4 fortune cookie. Highest rating.
jokes.setup: Peng Lee is giving his movie rating
jokes.punchline: The nonsensical 'fortune cookie' rating system
jokes.explanation: Relies on ethnic stereotyping for humor - the absurdist rating system has some merit but the foundation is problematic

dialogue.speaker: Peng Lee
dialogue.line: I give it 3 fortune... 4 fortune cookie. Highest rating.

jokes.text: What is it with the Chinese guy? He's on once a week and he's not funny. I know. Why don't you tell Larry? I'm not gonna tell him.
jokes.setup: Hank and Beverly watching the Peng Lee segment
jokes.punchline: Their honest assessment and reluctance to tell Larry the truth
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on the problematic segment - shows characters' awareness while highlighting workplace dynamics

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: What is it with the Chinese guy? He's on once a week and he's not funny.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I know.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Why don't you tell Larry?

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I'm not gonna tell him.

jokes.text: Very bad for hemorrhoid.
jokes.setup: Peng Lee mentions the broken theater seat
jokes.punchline: His oversharing about personal medical issues
jokes.explanation: Cheap TMI humor that relies more on the broken English delivery than clever writing

dialogue.speaker: Peng Lee
dialogue.line: Very bad for hemorrhoid.

jokes.text: I can't do this with Larry watching me. It gives me the creeps. Doing it in front of my clients turns me on. I feel my power.
jokes.setup: Paula is uncomfortable with Larry watching while she's intimate with Stevie
jokes.punchline: Stevie's creepy admission that performing for clients is a turn-on
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Stevie's narcissistic, power-hungry character while creating genuine discomfort

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: I can't do this with Larry watching me. It gives me the creeps.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Doing it in front of my clients turns me on. I feel my power.

jokes.text: Yeah, but I, uh, suspect lately our pork chop dishes, they suffer. Nobody order pork chop dishes.
jokes.setup: Larry asks if Peng Lee enjoyed the talking pig movie Babe
jokes.punchline: Peng Lee connects the movie to his restaurant's declining pork sales
jokes.explanation: Actually clever observation about how the movie might affect restaurant business - works despite the problematic character

dialogue.speaker: Peng Lee
dialogue.line: Yeah, but I, uh, suspect lately our pork chop dishes, they suffer. Nobody order pork chop dishes.

jokes.text: Oh, man, this is hilarious, don't you think? Artie? Don't you think this is hilarious? It's terrific. I'm enjoying it.
jokes.setup: Larry is fishing for validation about the Peng Lee segment
jokes.punchline: Artie's obviously forced, unenthusiastic agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Larry's insecurity and Artie's diplomatic dishonesty

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Oh, man, this is hilarious, don't you think? Artie? Don't you think this is hilarious? It's terrific. I'm enjoying it.

jokes.text: Tell her to clean around me.
jokes.setup: Beverly tells Larry the housekeeper is there and he needs to get up
jokes.punchline: Larry's lazy, entitled request to have the cleaning done around his sleeping form
jokes.explanation: Perfectly encapsulates Larry's selfishness and entitlement in four words

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Tell her to clean around me.

jokes.text: Yes, sweetie, I do. I'm an addict.
jokes.setup: Beverly asks if Larry has to smoke immediately upon waking
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact admission of his addiction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Larry's blunt honesty about his flaws is both funny and revealing

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Yes, sweetie, I do. I'm an addict.

jokes.text: Fuck him, he doesn't know. He's the one who swore ovitz wasn't leaving.
jokes.setup: Stevie is on a business call, agitated about industry drama
jokes.punchline: The specific reference to someone's wrong prediction about Michael Ovitz
jokes.explanation: Good industry insider reference that shows Stevie's world of Hollywood deal-making

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Fuck him, he doesn't know. He's the one who swore ovitz wasn't leaving.

jokes.text: This whole disney thing has everybody shitting bricks, pins, and party favors.
jokes.setup: Stevie explaining why everyone is stressed
jokes.punchline: The escalation from 'shitting bricks' to include Disney-themed items
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of a common phrase with Disney-specific items - creative and memorable

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: This whole disney thing has everybody shitting bricks, pins, and party favors.

jokes.text: Of course not. I already told you I'm doing this for 5 years and then I'm getting out. You're so deluded. OK. 8 years.
jokes.setup: Paula asks if Stevie will be like this forever
jokes.punchline: Stevie immediately extends his timeline when called deluded
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Stevie's self-deception and inability to commit to change

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Of course not. I already told you I'm doing this for 5 years and then I'm getting out.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: You're so deluded.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: OK. 8 years.

jokes.text: That's just the kind of Hollywood bullshit that makes me want to Chuck it all and join the peace corps. Yeah? Big bad Ron, you piece of shit! How's it going? Oh, fuck you, asshole.
jokes.setup: Stevie complains about Hollywood phoniness and mentions wanting to join the Peace Corps
jokes.punchline: He immediately takes a call and engages in exactly the behavior he was criticizing
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing Stevie's complete lack of self-awareness - the immediate contradiction is perfectly timed

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: That's just the kind of Hollywood bullshit that makes me want to Chuck it all and join the peace corps. Yeah? Big bad Ron, you piece of shit! How's it going? Oh, fuck you, asshole.

jokes.text: Letterman can send 2 guys from bangladesh around the world and we can't send one Chinese guy to the movies?
jokes.setup: Larry defending the Peng Lee segment against criticism
jokes.punchline: His comparison to Letterman's 'Stupid Human Tricks' with international contestants
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's defensive rationalization while highlighting the problematic nature of both bits

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Letterman can send 2 guys from bangladesh around the world and we can't send one Chinese guy to the movies?

jokes.text: That's a... a parody of an asian-American judge, prancing around with twinkle-toed dinner theater rejects.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Leno's Judge Ito dancers
jokes.punchline: Dennis's elaborate, dismissive description of the bit
jokes.explanation: Vivid, specific description that effectively critiques the offensive nature of the Leno bit

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: That's a... a parody of an asian-American judge, prancing around with twinkle-toed dinner theater rejects.

jokes.text: Oh, and by, Dennis, by twinkle-toed, I imply nothing about their sexuality.
jokes.setup: Dennis just used the phrase 'twinkle-toed'
jokes.punchline: His immediate, unnecessary clarification about not implying anything sexual
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkward moment showing overly-PC awareness and creating more attention on what he was trying to avoid

dialogue.line: Oh, and by, Dennis, by twinkle-toed, I imply nothing about their sexuality.

jokes.text: I had his sweet and sour chicken the other day, it was like hot pink. I think it's cough syrup. I swear to God.
jokes.setup: Larry mentions eating at Peng Lee's restaurant
jokes.punchline: His gross description of the unnaturally colored food and comparison to cough syrup
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about questionable Chinese restaurant food quality

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I had his sweet and sour chicken the other day, it was like hot pink. I think it's cough syrup. I swear to God.

jokes.text: José jimenez was the first hispanic in space. Kids looked up to him. Well, he didn't actually go into space. That was just an act. Well, I believed it.
jokes.setup: Artie defends old ethnic comedy characters as inspirational
jokes.punchline: His admission that he actually believed the fictional character went to space
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Artie's generational naivety and simple worldview - both touching and funny

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: José jimenez was the first hispanic in space. Kids looked up to him. Well, he didn't actually go into space. That was just an act. Well, I believed it.

jokes.text: Well, then, you'll have a lot of screaming Japs picketing outside the studio. Screaming asian-americans.
jokes.setup: Dennis warning Larry about consequences of continuing the Peng Lee bit
jokes.punchline: His slip into offensive language followed by immediate PC correction
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable but realistic portrayal of workplace casual racism and performative correction

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Well, then, you'll have a lot of screaming Japs picketing outside the studio. Screaming asian-americans.

jokes.text: I sent Brian to the bank with my atm card, but I don't know what happened to him. He probably got sucked into some kind of parade.
jokes.setup: Hank explaining why he can't pay Phil back
jokes.punchline: His absurd theory about Brian getting distracted by a random parade
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank moment - the specific, absurd explanation for a mundane situation captures his character beautifully

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I sent Brian to the bank with my atm card, but I don't know what happened to him. He probably got sucked into some kind of parade.

jokes.text: It cured my cold.
jokes.setup: Hank mentions ordering Peng Lee's sweet and sour chicken
jokes.punchline: Claims the questionable food actually had medicinal benefits
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that adds to the running gag about Peng Lee's restaurant food

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: It cured my cold.

jokes.text: What, is he only taking 10% of your love?
jokes.setup: Phil asking about Paula's relationship with her agent boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Connecting agent commission percentages to romantic relationships
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures the business/personal relationship dynamic

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: What, is he only taking 10% of your love?

jokes.text: I want it to continue the way that it is except with more fucking.
jokes.setup: Paula asks what Stevie wants from their relationship
jokes.punchline: His blunt, reductive answer about wanting more sex
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Stevie's emotional immaturity and inability to handle serious relationship talk

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I want it to continue the way that it is except with more fucking.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, are you pregnant? No, but it's nice to see what your reaction would be.
jokes.setup: Paula mentions having a baby hypothetically
jokes.punchline: Her revelation that she was testing his reaction, and his panicked response
jokes.explanation: Good relationship dynamic showing Paula's testing behavior and Stevie's commitment phobia

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Oh, my God, are you pregnant?

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: No, but it's nice to see what your reaction would be.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sure he'd be so thrilled to hear about Ed begley Jr.'s fucking electric car.
jokes.setup: Stevie threatens to take his clients to Letterman
jokes.punchline: Paula's sarcastic dismissal of Ed Begley Jr.'s eco-friendly car topic as boring TV
jokes.explanation: Sharp industry joke about the inherent boringness of certain celebrity talking points

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm sure he'd be so thrilled to hear about Ed begley Jr.'s fucking electric car.

jokes.text: she, uh, got a really bad cold and she can't make it. I see, a cold. Yeah, it's pretty serious.
jokes.setup: Paula gives an obviously fake excuse for Jennifer Aniston's cancellation
jokes.punchline: Her doubling down on the 'serious cold' story
jokes.explanation: Good awkward lying moment, though the setup is fairly predictable

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: she, uh, got a really bad cold and she can't make it.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I see, a cold.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Yeah, it's pretty serious.

jokes.text: She was skiing in Aspen and she, uh, got a really bad cold / and she can't make it. / I see, a cold. / Yeah, it's pretty serious.
jokes.setup: Paula making up excuse for Jennifer Aniston cancellation
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate lies about severity of a cold
jokes.explanation: Good workplace lying scenario. Paula digging herself deeper while Artie's skepticism is palpable.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: She was skiing in Aspen and she, uh, got a really bad cold / and she can't make it.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I see, a cold.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Yeah, it's pretty serious.

jokes.text: The weather's so unpredictable this time of year when there's no snow.
jokes.setup: Paula's lie about Jennifer Aniston skiing and getting sick in Aspen
jokes.punchline: Artie's dry observation about the impossibility of skiing without snow
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie moment - calling out the lie with deadpan logic while maintaining plausible deniability

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: The weather's so unpredictable this time of year when there's no snow.

jokes.text: Well, maybe you don't recognize me 'cause I'm a comic who never gets on TV. And why? Well, basically, thanks to people like you, that's why.
jokes.setup: Paula doesn't recognize Andy Kindler when he approaches
jokes.punchline: His bitter, direct accusation about why he's not famous
jokes.explanation: Good bitter comic character moment - captures the desperation and blame-shifting of struggling performers

dialogue.speaker: Andy Kindler
dialogue.line: Well, maybe you don't recognize me 'cause I'm a comic who never gets on TV. And why? Well, basically, thanks to people like you, that's why.

jokes.text: My advice? Pepper spray, and don't be afraid to use it.
jokes.setup: Phil explains he can't go to ATMs after dark because he gets mugged
jokes.punchline: Hank's casual advice about self-defense instead of helping with the money
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hank's selfishness and tendency to give unhelpful advice

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: My advice? Pepper spray, and don't be afraid to use it.

jokes.text: Jennifer sprained her ankle. Really? You know, according to Paula, she mentioned to Artie that Jennifer has a head cold.
jokes.setup: Stevie and Paula are giving conflicting excuses for the same cancellation
jokes.punchline: Larry pointing out the inconsistency in their lies
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how lies multiply and contradict each other

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Jennifer sprained her ankle.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Really? You know, according to Paula, she mentioned to Artie that Jennifer has a head cold.

jokes.text: OK, Hank, give him the money. What money? Hey, there it is. What are you... all you had to do was ask. Prick.
jokes.setup: Phil can't get his money back from Hank
jokes.punchline: Larry apparently forcing Hank to produce the money, followed by Hank's petulant response
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment showing workplace hierarchy and Hank's childish behavior

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: OK, Hank, give him the money.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: What money?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Hey, there it is.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: What are you... all you had to do was ask.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Prick.

jokes.text: I walk around in my Italian suit all day like I'm on top of the world, but I'm a fake! And if I stop pretending for a second, I fall apart 'cause I'm terrified! I don't know what the fuck I'm doing.
jokes.setup: Stevie is having an emotional breakdown
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top confession about his insecurity and fraudulent feelings
jokes.explanation: Good vulnerable character moment that reveals Stevie's deeper insecurities beneath his bravado

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I walk around in my Italian suit all day like I'm on top of the world, but I'm a fake! And if I stop pretending for a second, I fall apart 'cause I'm terrified! I don't know what the fuck I'm doing.

jokes.text: Tell her about the suit.
jokes.setup: Stevie just had an emotional breakdown about his Italian suit
jokes.punchline: Larry's focus on the most superficial detail of the confession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - missing the emotional point and focusing on the material detail

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Tell her about the suit.

jokes.text: He is lame. Everybody in town thinks so. He's not funny.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Peng Lee's comedy segments
jokes.punchline: Character acknowledging what audience has been thinking
jokes.explanation: Meta moment where character voices audience's opinion. Good resolution of the running Peng Lee problem through honest assessment.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: He is lame. Everybody in town thinks so. He's not funny.

jokes.text: Larry is the gorilla. He's huge, talent-wise.
jokes.setup: Jennifer Aniston doesn't understand the King Kong reference from earlier
jokes.punchline: Stevie's literal explanation of his metaphor
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier sycophantic moment, shows Stevie's continued ass-kissing behavior

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Larry is the gorilla. He's huge, talent-wise.

jokes.text: Larry's the king, man. King Kong! / Ha ha ha ha! What does that mean? / Where does an 800-pound gorilla sleep? / Anywhere he wants to. / I don't get it.
jokes.setup: Hank's enthusiastic endorsement of Larry
jokes.punchline: Larry's complete confusion at the compliment structure
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Hank's confused enthusiasm meets Larry's literal-mindedness. The callback to Stevie's earlier 'King Kong' reference adds layers.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Larry's the king, man. King Kong!

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Ha ha ha ha! What does that mean?

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Where does an 800-pound gorilla sleep?

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Anywhere he wants to.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I don't get it.

jokes.text: Um, I'm... gosh, but I can't really think of anything I'd like to ask you at the moment. If I come up with something, I'll ask you later.
jokes.setup: Larry asks Jennifer what she'd like to ask them
jokes.punchline: Her complete inability to come up with anything, followed by the awkward promise to call later
jokes.explanation: Good awkward talk show moment showing the artificial nature of celebrity interviews

dialogue.speaker: Jennifer Aniston
dialogue.line: Um, I'm... gosh, but I can't really think of anything I'd like to ask you at the moment. If I come up with something, I'll ask you later.

jokes.text: Hey, you... you didn't get back together with me just so you could get Jennifer aniston back on the show, did you? Of course not.
jokes.setup: Paula and Stevie got back together right when they needed Jennifer Aniston
jokes.punchline: Paula's suspicious question and Stevie's obviously false denial
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending moment that questions the sincerity of their reconciliation and Stevie's motives

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Hey, you... you didn't get back together with me just so you could get Jennifer aniston back on the show, did you?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Of course not.
